entities.name: Self-reflection
entities.context: Cognitive activity subject to high activation energy barrier; central subject of this position

entities.name: Activation energy barrier
entities.context: Framework describing psychological resistance to initiating effortful tasks

entities.name: Hyperbolic discounting
entities.context: Cognitive bias where future utility is undervalued relative to immediate costs; applied here to identity work

entities.name: Just-in-time interventions
entities.context: Recommended design approach for career tools that captures users at moments of necessity

entities.name: Just-in-case platforms
entities.context: Contrasted design approach that relies on abstract future readiness; deemed less effective

entities.name: Narrative extraction
entities.context: Expensive cognitive task involved in self-reflection

entities.context: Expensive cognitive task involved in self-reflection

entities.name: Identity work

entities.name: Career tools
entities.context: Products designed for career development that must account for reflection inertia

entities.name: Interview scheduling
entities.context: Example of high-stakes workflow that creates forcing function for reflection

relationships.context: Self-reflection requires overcoming high activation energy that utility alone cannot provide
Resistance to self-reflection is a manifestation of hyperbolic discounting applied to identity work
V believes career tools should be designed as just-in-time interventions attached to high-stakes workflows

Interview scheduling cited as example high-stakes workflow to attach interventions to
